message.content: Endpoint Denial of Service (DoS) attacks aim to degrade or block the availability of services by exhausting system resources or causing persistent crashes. These attacks can target various layers of the application stack, including operating systems, server applications, and web-based applications. Adversaries may use techniques such as IP address spoofing and botnets to conduct these attacks, making it difficult to trace and filter malicious traffic. Endpoint DoS attacks can be politically motivated or used to support other malicious activities like distraction, hacktivism, and extortion. High-profile examples include the 2012 DDoS attacks on major US banks and the 2019 disruption of Georgian websites by the Sandworm Team. Mitigation strategies include implementing rate limiting, using web application firewalls, and deploying anti-DDoS services to detect and block malicious traffic. Regularly updating and patching systems can also help reduce vulnerabilities that adversaries might exploit.
